page: Amazon Web Services r8id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is supported by a substantial 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Additionally, the instance includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8id.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance characteristics, particularly in single-core operations. Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing scores rank in the top tier, representing the top 10% of tested systems. The server also shows strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, encryption, physics, and Redis SET operations, which reached 58.74 million operations per second. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 388.5 tokens per second on small 135M models, alongside strong prompt processing speeds for both small and medium models. Memory bandwidth and multi-core stress tests perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8id.8xlarge offers a high-density resource profile by bundling massive DDR5 memory capacity with fast local NVMe SSD storage. This integration reduces the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput workloads, though the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel deep learning training. The hardware configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive and high-I/O applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, large-scale data decompression, web serving, and local LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier single-core performance, image processing, and text processing. It also exhibits strong performance in Redis SET operations, encryption, and small-model LLM text generation. The combination of high-capacity DDR5 RAM and bundled local NVMe storage makes this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads without requiring external storage. It is ideally suited for in-memory databases, data compression, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
